assistant_output_excerpt:
Validating local boot first with secrets sourced but not printed.


The first script hit a zsh variable naming issue, not an app failure. Retrying with a safe variable name.


Local boot verified. Moving to Docker build.


Docker build found a missing native dependency for Psych (`libyaml`). Fixing Dockerfile and continuing.


Retrying Docker build after adding libyaml dependencies.


Docker build now passes. Running Docker Compose with sourced environment and verifying the container responds.


Compose build/run succeeded and the app responded. I’m doing final hygiene checks after the Dockerfile fix.


Running one minimal streaming request with the sourced OpenRouter environment to verify RubyLLM is wired, without printing any secret values.


RubyLLM streaming smoke test passed. Finalizing.


Checking final container state.


Validation complete; no compose containers left running.


Phase 2 complete.

Validated in order:

- Local Rails app boots and responds on `/up` and `/`
- RubyLLM streaming endpoint smoke-tested with `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` sourced from `~/.config/zsh/secrets`
- Fixed Docker build failure by adding `libyaml-dev` to build deps and `libyaml-0-2` to runtime deps
- `docker build -t rubyllm-chat-phase2 .` finishes successfully
- `docker compose up --build -d` builds, starts, and responds on `/up` and `/`
- Compose stack was stopped afterward
- Final checks still pass:
  - `bin/rails test`
  - `bin/rubocop`

No secrets were printed or written into project files.
command:
Once finished coding and testing, you must make sure it runs, both locally, and that docker build also finishes successfully, and docker compose is able to run.

Do not ask for confirmation. Do not stop with a plan. Fulfill the request and finish.

This is phase 2 of 2 and a continuation of the existing project in the current working directory. Do not regenerate the app. Do not create a second app. Validate and fix the current codebase in place.

Use environment variables so RubyLLM actually works. Source ~/.config/zsh/secrets only to load the required environment, especially OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Do not print secret values, do not copy secret file contents into the repo, and do not commit secrets into files, logs, Dockerfiles, compose files, or README examples.

Validate the project end-to-end in this order:
- run the local app in a way that proves it boots
- make any fixes needed so it boots cleanly
- make sure `docker build` finishes successfully
- make sure `docker compose up --build` is able to run

If one step passes, move to the next without asking for confirmation. If something fails, fix it and continue. Only stop when you have actually completed the validation work or you hit a hard blocker you cannot resolve from inside the workspace.

model.label: Qwen3.7 Max
model.selection_reason: Alibaba Qwen3.7 Max on OpenRouter. Added 2026-06-15 to run + score in-house after community PR #4 submitted pre-scored results (82/A on a phase-2 DNF) without the gitignored project code. Tests whether the flagship Qwen Max tier achieves correct RubyLLM API usage and completes phase 2 validation under our harness.
